briefing.headline: Rig up a Football Jig — finesse fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Football Jig is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Hackney: 'Use a 3/4 to 1-ounce football head jig on 12-17 pound fluorocarbon to cover water and locate sweet spots along gravel bars or ridges with isolated stumps. Same slow presentation as a Carolina rig but only one knot.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Hackney: 'Use a 3/4 to 1-ounce football head jig on 12-17 pound fluorocarbon to cover water and locate sweet spots along gravel bars or ridges with isolated stumps. Same slow presentation as a Carolin
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, rock, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Yama Craw buoyancy makes claws float up behind football jig in defensive crawfish posture. Perfect for bottom-hopping presentations
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, rock, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: When fishing a football jig on deep rock, make a long cast, let it hit bottom, and drag it slowly — most bites come when the bait pops off a rock as bass follow it on the drag

Robertson: A wind direction change can destroy a deep-water pattern by muddying previously clear water. When visibility drops from 6 feet to 2 feet, bass stop moving through the area and the deep bite dies. Be ready to switch to a shallow backup p...
A stick bait on a skirtless football head is a productive look for walking down bluff ends

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal fall fish pull tighter to cover and deeper structure at 8-18ft. The shad migration pauses but doesn't stop. Focus on the deepest fish in a given creek arm — channel bends and main creek points.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
